It is a very nice family place, which needs to have some renovation indoors and outdoors, update the seats and tables, give more a new look ambiance with the Mediterranean Beach breath taking view...
I would definitely come again

address: Thoum Seaside Road, Batroun, Batroun District, Batroun 1400 Lebanon
review_text:

address: Thoum Seaside Road, Batroun, Batroun District, Batroun 1400 Lebanon
review_text:
We feel like we are between our family and friends when we go there.
The food is so tasty the music is very nice and the staff are super friendly.

This is my first bad review but I guess it is necessary. I’ve always enjoyed your restaurant, but our recent visit was very disappointing. I received my salad half an hour before my husband’s order, so I had to eat alone. When his salmon finally arrived, the mashed potatoes were cold. The chef dismissed our concern, saying that’s how it’s served, though the manager later agreed with us. Unfortunately, we left hungry and frustrated, with no apology or gesture to make up for the experience.

review_title: Mashed potatoes cannot be served hot
review_text:
I went with my wife and we ordered 2 plates, one crispy salmon and one Grilled Salmon Pineapple.

The grilled salmon arrived half an hr after the crispy salmon, their reason was it takes more time (you can start cooking the one that takes time first). The crispy salmon has mashed potatoes with it which arrived cold as if it was in the fridge (the chef's reason was that it can't be heated because it has milk, so it is not freshly done which is a worse outcome).

The end result worst experience and full bill paid.
Great food with a creative twist and kind service, beautiful sea view right at the beach. Highly recommend this fun and simple place for chill and enjoyment.

This restaurant is truly a gem! The staff are incredibly welcoming and attentive, and the food is absolutely delicious. Every dish is full of flavor and beautifully served. Can’t wait to come back again! Special thanks to Charbel, ask for him once you're there
The atmosphere was absolutely amazing, the  staff extremely kind. The food was incredible, terrific presentation. The portion size was very generous! Ask for Charbel, he is super accommodating!

Starbucks batroun is one of the best places I've ever felt like my home, they have a great team and they love their work, and I pay special thanks to an employee named Hani, who has the energy and the constant smile.

restaurant_name: Meat The Fish At The Beach

Authentic Lebanese food cooked by local women, super tasty and fresh! The prices are so reasonable too. Anyone going to Batroun should definitely try. The owner is so sweet and everything is on point.

address: Batrouniyat St, Batroun | Next to Dagher Pharmacy, Batroun 0000 Lebanon

The best experience

address: Batrouniyat St, Batroun | Next to Dagher Pharmacy, Batroun 0000 Lebanon
review_text:
I recently visited Mentabkhetna restaurant, and it was truly a great experience. From the warm and friendly greeting at the door to every dish that we ordered.
First, the atmosphere was cozy and the seating arrangements were comfortable.
We started things off with a few cold mezzas. Hummus was so creamy, baba ghanouj was flavorful, and the tabbouleh was refreshing.
For the main course, it was served hot and fresh, and the portions were generous.
Overall, I can't recommend Mentabkhetna enough. If you're looking for authentic and innovative Lebanese cuisine in a welcoming and intimate setting, this is the spot for you. I can't wait to return and try more of their delicious dishes.

Good service
Fair prices
Average food

But SMOKING was everywhere indoor

review_title: Nice Place with average experience of Food
review_text:
The place is really nice, traditional and authentic.
Prices are affordable and portions are ok.
The food taste is average with some new names for different products like Sambousik shrimps, it turned to be Samosa.
The white wine was not tasty at all.

We had lunch at batrouniyat me and my husband and it was an amazing one. Food quality was great, cleanliness was super and service was so. Food was very presentable and generous,staff so friendly and welcoming. Washroom was clean and overall the atmosphere was so relaxing and comfy. 
Recommend!!
Nice place...like the outdoor and the indoor especially...staff ok but very slow service...food ok...there is a place for the children to play...price fair enough

A great spacious location with bug potentials, YET very poorly managed.
Batrouniat is a dull experience with little to offer. 
Domestic home made products, nice to shop but unjustifiably expensive. The cafeteria has little to nothing to present.
The management needs a shuffle.

The restaurant is very expensive, as a lebanese who like to enjoy lebanese food we were very disappointed the whole family.
We ate the worth sanboussik, hommos and kebbe...
I will neved recommand this restaurant to someone and it was a very bad experience for Douma, we just wanted to leave this village :(

Douma in March is pretty quiet, and restaurant options are limited if staying in town. Fortunately Chalhoub was open. Although we ended up being the only diners on a dark and cold evening, the reception was warm and the food good, We tried the fattoush and grilled shrimp, and washed it down with Beirut Beer. Everything was good, and fairly priced.

A Very simple place where it is preferred to reserve because the place is usually busy.
It is a family business where you can find a nice spirit and good smiles.
As for food, it is tasty and fresh.
Portions are fair and prices are fair and affordable.

my family loved the drive there ... we had high hopes but the cuisine came very disappointing.
the service was friendly though

review_title: good place for lunch, nice food and pleasant service
review_text: I had lunch twice in this beautiful family run fish restaurant. A nice place, good food and friendly service. Prices are good.

address: Eddeh Road Opposite Petrol Station, Batroun, Lebanon, Batroun Lebanon
review_text:
Visited while I was on holiday. After the first visit, I became a regular and would eat there every other day if not everyday.

The food is excellent and clean. The ingredients are fresh and most of the vegetables are home grown.

The restaurant is family run and feels very welcoming.

I even asked if they do catering for birthdays and other occasions, which they do.

I would definitely recommend eating here!

My experience at Chez Maguy Batroun was terrible—from the service to the food and even the way it was served. The price was far too high for what we received. I definitely wouldn’t recommend this place to anyone.

review_title: Disappointing Dining Experience at Chez Maguy
review_text:
Our visit to Chez Maguy in Batroun was a letdown from start to finish. The tabbouleh was overwhelmingly salty, the batata harra and fried fish were undercooked, and the grilled shrimp hadn’t been cleaned, leaving us to do it ourselves at the table. On top of that, the service was frustrating—an older gentleman from the staff took our salad plate before we were done and insisted it was finished, despite our objection.

We also ordered a grilled fish based on their recommendation, and while the choice itself was good, we were shocked when the bill arrived—$40 for a single piece of fish. Between the poorly prepared food, the dismissive service, and the unexpected price tag, this was not an experience I’d want to repeat or recommend.

I should have listened to the other reviewers on trip advisor!! Although we have been to this place off season and sat outside with a beautiful view- this time around, we were extremely disappointed and actually embarrassed in front of our good friends! The food was mediocre to say the least- extremely expensive for the quality and type of fish. We asked for grilled, the small fish came out basking in some oil, smelling and was not good whatsoever. She charged 78$ for 400gms of fish!!!! What a rip off!!! The tahini sauce was watered down and tasteless🥺 the octopus was so mushy we returned it! No desert- nothing. We will never set foot in this place again.

i had very bad experience at this restaurant. it was almost empty ( almost 4tables only) and yet we had to call for someone to take our order.service was very slow, unprofessional, not friendly, with attitide and arrogance. food was not generous and it was average. coffee was served in rakwa not enough for us as 4 but they charged us for 4 cups of coffee! and to rop it all, they charged us twice some items on their bill other than the lunch we had was extremely overpriced!! we will never go back there!
It was an amazing experience with the best waiter Ali!

I had a wonderful experience at Swiss Butter. The service was outstanding, with everything managed thoughtfully and attentively. A special thanks to our waitress, Aquilina, whose professionalism, helpfulness, and genuine kindness truly stood out. Her warm and attentive approach added an extra touch that made our visit even more delightful.

review_title: A lovely lunch at swiss butter
review_text:
I had a great experience at Swiss Butter. The service was excellent, and everything was handled with care and attention. A special mention goes to our waitress, Aquilina, who was not only professional and helpful, but also genuinely kind and attentive throughout my visit. Her warm attitude made the dining experience even more enjoyable.

Highly recommend this place for both the quality of service and the welcoming atmosphere.

I had one of the worst dining experiences ever at Swiss Butter in Batroun. The food was mediocre at best, but the real problem was the dishonest way they handle orders. The waitress recommended a ribeye that wasn’t even on the menu and never told us the price — which turned out to be a shocking $55 per plate. We ordered it twice, still never being informed of the cost, only to find out at the end when the bill came. This is deceptive, unethical, and unacceptable. Any restaurant with integrity tells customers the price before selling something, especially when it’s outrageously overpriced. The service was disappointing, the experience left a bad taste in every way, and I will warn others so they don’t get ripped off the way we did.

gmitri843@gmail.com

Location so nice, looking by the see.
Staff so friendly, and food tasty but limited menu.
Good atmosphere for a dinner during sunset

We were a group of 14 people and unfortunately none of us enjoyed the lunch. The service was very poor, and the food quality was unacceptable.

The fish (El Bizri) tasted bitter and seemed like it had been prepared the day before.

The grilled fish was unappetizing.

The kabab was still raw.

Hygiene was completely lacking: dishes, forks, and spoons even fell on the floor and were placed back on the table.

To make matters worse, no apology was offered. One staff member even admitted we were right, yet nothing was done to correct the situation.

Overall, it was a very disappointing experience, and I would not recommend this restaurant.

review_title: Beautiful and quiet terrace on the sea. Good Mezze
review_text: Nice spot on a wooden terrace on the sea. Gentle wind cooling down the heat. A little bit apart from the other bustling restaurants of Batroun. Good food and service.

review_title: Worst service, worst food quality, worst management

review_text: Poor food quality, small portions that don't illustrate at all the Lebanese hospitality, high prices that definitely don't match with the poor quality of food (oily fries, etc.) and two of the seafood dishes we had ordered from the menu were not available (they've stopped serving them but did not bother removing them from the menu). The menu is quite limited and some prices are very high for no reason  (35 USD for a dish of raw fish) We went there mostly to enjoy the nice sea view, and the music was nice, but apart from.that, it is one of these overrated  "cool" places that is definitely not worth it.

It is located in a small street on the other side of the town, so there is no view for the whole place. If I am going to eat in Batroun, or even drink a great lemonade, and have good chat with my friends or family, this restaurant is going to be my must, but actually there is nothing unique about this restaurant beside its great food. Fries and burger were both cold. Not even a little bit warm. Just cold. Meat wasn’t decently cooked. More like raw. Very disappointing food. No care at all. First and last time ordering.

So tender it melts in your mouth!!! The best part is the chef’s secret spices and sauce that give it a unique and amazing flavor. A MUST TASTE!!!
As an amateur of Burger, i strongly suggest to try this spot.. freshly ground meat that makes you question any other burger

They also have Orso Bianco's ice cream🤩

review_title: "Exorbitant Prices and Slow Service Mar Otherwise Promising Venue"
review_text:
The venue boasts a commendable location, strategically positioned near the highway in northern Lebanon, not far from the city of Batroun. They offer an electric cart service to transport guests from the entrance to the beach, where an array of comfortable tables and chairs are available. The service, albeit courteous, is noticeably slow. The DJ requires a considerable amount of time to play invigorating music; initially, the music was exceedingly monotonous, only improving after 10:00 PM when livelier tunes finally emerged. Unfortunately, by this time, a significant portion of my friends had already left, disheartened by the earlier dull atmosphere. 

The food, while decent, is exorbitantly priced. A bottle of gin, which typically costs $25, was outrageously marked up to $128. Our modest consumption of some snacks and soft drinks, along with the gin, culminated in a staggering bill of $462. The pricing is egregiously inflated, posing a severe detriment to tourism. In numerous other countries with far superior infrastructure (such as roads, cleanliness, and service) — like Spain, France, Italy, Greece, Kazakhstan, and even Dubai — the maximum expenditure per person, including excellent food and beverages, would be approximately $40. Adding insult to injury, the valet parking demanded an additional $5.50 as a fee. In many countries, parking is complimentary when dining at a restaurant.

In Lebanon, it seems exploitation is rampant, extending beyond just the banks and political system. I intend to boycott all restaurants and pubs this summer and strongly urge others to do the same to compel these establishments to reevaluate their prices and cease their exploitative practices.

review_title: "Extremely Disappointing Reservation Experience at Butlers: Unfulfilled Promises and Poor Service"
review_text: "Extremely disappointed with my recent reservation experience at Butlers on June 1. We paid 50% in advance and received confirmation for three tables with 12 seats. However, upon arrival, our tables were given away. After a long wait, we were seated near the restrooms with no resolution. The entire staff, including the unhelpful manager, failed to address our concerns. This falls far short of the promises made during the booking process. We hope Butlers takes this feedback seriously and improves their service for future guests. #dissatisfiedcustomer #poorcustomerservice #butlersrestaurant"

I hated this place. First, it’s hard to find it easily because the beach is a part of a hotel so no no sign for the beach there is only a sign for the hotel which has a different name. The pool is little bit bigger than a bathtub. We were 10 people and they put us in a place for 5 places although we had a reservation and there was plenty of places next to the beach. Food is average not generous at all at high price. I don’t recommend. Batroun has plenty of beaches much better than this one.
Best place in Batroun. Excellent food, music, drinks & vibes. Amazing sunset experience. Amazing service

The service, ambience, crowd and food is amazing. It is a very elegant and upscale venue. I would definitely recommend it for a great beach day or evening dinner and drinks. Make sure you have a reservation though.

review_title: Rude Service and Poor Quality at Oysters – Never Again
review_text:
I had a very disappointing experience at Oysters this year. We ordered grilled calamari and were served only the tentacles instead of the actual calamari, which absolutely did not match the price. When we complained, both the waiter and the manager were extremely rude, insisting “this is how we serve it.” We even showed them the restaurant’s own Instagram photos, which clearly showed a completely different dish, but the manager dismissed us by saying those pictures are just for advertising.

I visited Oysters back in 2023 and had a wonderful experience — but this time, it was the complete opposite. The fish were tasteless, the fried shrimps were served with the heads and not cleaned properly, and the calamari was a total disaster. Not only did they refuse to apologize, but the manager was unprofessional, impolite, and even threw some unnecessary words at us before walking away.

We didn’t mind paying for the dish, but we simply didn’t want it. Instead of handling the situation with professionalism, the management’s attitude made everything worse. Honestly, this visit wasn’t even worth one star.

My friend and I stumbled on this restaurant by accident. We had gone to see the Phoenician wall, and we parked right in front of the restaurant just by the sea.
The staff were very nice, especially a young lady, I forgot to ask her name. They took their time to explain the dishes to us. We had Fattouch, tabbouleh, and grilled pig fish. The food was very nice, as was the lemonade.
Oysters are their specialty, so I decided to have one (my first time ever). I am sure I will try it again.

review_title: IF IN BATROUN AND YOU SEE A RESTAURANT CALLED OYSTERS THST LOOKS SO INVITING FOR A SUNSET, DO NOT GO THERE!
review_text:
My last night in Lebanon, I go to watch the sunset and see this charming seafood place called Oysters near St Estephan Church.
I ask for oysters, the one server who spoke English said they didn’t have any, then said he would check. 10 minutes later he says yes, they have 6. I’ve eaten oysters all over the world and this place looked safe…..but it is NOT.
The oysters looked funny and had a white substance dripping off them. The server told me it was “the sauce they put on them”. Sauce? What? So I tried one with the least milky stuff on it and it tasted rancid. I spit it out into a napkin. 
I told the staff they are bad, and these were likely sitting out for hours.
They insisted I had to pay, and get this, they were 5 USDs an oyster!!! No oysters in the US are that expensive.
I was told to wait for the owner. 30 minutes later he walks up and I explained what happened. The 2 servers who served me ran off in their car. Apparently another oyster was missing. I swore I did not even eat the one and spit it out. 
He told me there was nothing he could do, he must pay for each oyster. I told him if he went to a restaurant and the food was bad, would he pay? He said “Of course”
WRONG!!!!
AVOID THIS PLACE! Maybe I was naive and stupid to try and eat oysters in Lebanon, but I have eaten many places and should’ve really been more careful, but just as a warning to others….DO NOT EAT HERE!
I usually write good reviews, but this could be one of my worst ones ever!

This is a family run establishment with all of the food prepared on the premises. review_text: Lovely atmosphere by the Phoenician Walls. The menu has everything you need. They serve the dishes in shells try to get a table upstairs if it's not so hot and you will have a meal with the most magical view.

review_title: Such good food - and so fresh!
review_text: An unassuming street café serving seafood wraps - you could walk straight past it and not know what you'd missed - but oh! The food! So fresh its almost swimming off your plate - tasty and excellently cooked - just perfect. You could pay ten times this price in a posh seafood restaurant and not have a tenth of the pleasure at the taste. Thanks all at the café - such nice food (and local craft beer available as well!)

I visited this small fish sandwich shop with a couple of friends and all three of us ordered a second sandwich immediately after we finished the first ones. All ingredients are fresh, super tasty, the place is clean and the owner is very friendly. It’s located in old souk that is very beautiful itself, close to the beach. I’ll definitely be back every time I visit Batroun.

Staff Expertise: 9/10, Very polite and helpful, they will definitely make some helpful suggestions regarding the food. Especially considering we had dietary requirements. 

• Atmosphere: 10/10, The atmosphere was amazing especially the upstairs and outside area. Felt like we were in Italy and the seats are comfortable. Also the background music is very well selected.

• Food & Drinks: 10/10, The food here is amazing honestly, menu item portion size was perfect (not small servings like other Lebanese restaurants) the food quality is very satisfying.

• Cleanliness: 10/10, Tables and dining areas are clean, as well as the  bathroom, had a lovely scent.

Highly recommended

review_title: One of the best restaurants in Batroun
review_text: Excellent food one of the best i have every time that i am in Batroun. Plus it has different kind of dishes not the one u find in every restaurant and they are all yummy.
We had a really great experience here. The staff were fantastic. The food was delicious. We had linguini and risotto, which were both fantastic. Accompanied by a fantastic bottle of Lebanese wine.

This seafood restaurant stood out as a culinary revelation that defied our expectations. Despite initially being overshadowed by a couple of other higher-ranked seafood restaurants in the area, we decided to give Bistr’eau a try as our first meal, upon arriving in Batroun. Little did we know, it would turn out to be the culinary highlight of our stay, ultimately the place we selected as our last meal and the grand finale of our time in Batroun.

Situated beneath a series of picturesque succession of ornate arches along the waterfront, within one of Batroun's historical buildings at the port of the Batroûn Old Souk, this restaurant stood out as a true outlier. What made this dining experience truly exceptional was the symphony of flavors orchestrated by the fusion of cuisines, the inventive pairing of traditional and rarely-used ingredients, and the meticulous execution of each dish. Between our two visits, we embarked on a culinary journey through almost the entire menu, relishing every morsel.

The appetizers alone were a testament to the culinary prowess at play – from the refreshing Fattouch to the innovative Hummus with shrimp, the smoky allure of the aubergine with sundried tomato tarator, and fresh zaatar. The culinary exploration continued with Grilled Calamari, Sicilian octopus, shrimp fokhara with tomato sauce, and perfectly roasted Patatas Bravas. All this food only served to pave the way for the triumphant main courses—seafood risotto and the pièce de résistance, a perfectly seasoned deep-fried whole fish. The culinary symphony reached its crescendo with the Pain Perdu à la mode, offering the perfect sweet note to conclude our culinary odyssey.
Amazing food, amazing place, amazing service. Batroon is out of this world. The restaurant is out of this world. I loved it and i highly recommend

review_title: Authentic and delicious in a traditional style batroun courtyard.
review_text: Excellent spot in the heart of Batroun. This used to be a house that was converted to a restaurant. They serve food that can be enjoyed any time of day. It’s operated by a family of 4. I have tried there saj manakeesh and the eggs cooked in the clay pot (fakhara) their bread is made on saj and served.

address: 1106 Old Souk Road Old Souk, Batroun 0000 Lebanon
review_text: We had breakfast twice here while staying in Batroun. The place is really laid back, with a yard with lemon trees and colorful tables, and also a veranda with sofas and low tables. The food is delicious and plenty, we were a group of six and ordered just different dishes to share. Elie and his family are very friendly and will answer every question you have. If we come to Batroun again we will definitely go back here!
